what_it_does: Implements the recollection-side memory retrieve for the Live Decision Surface: given a decision text (and optional subject address), it queries the shared corpus across configured spaces, optionally reranks the pooled hits, resolves human-readable meaning and provenance, and assembles a context bundle for the RHM. It also provides explanation_grounding, an explain-turn path with special framework-only handling and never-assert guardrails for theorem-fork decisions.
observations: Reuses existing machinery: suite.query_corpus, runtime.corpus_rerank.rerank_hits, runtime.corpus_neighbours.neighbours, and runtime.recall_determine.determine.
Optimizes the hot path by embedding the decision text once and taking one vector snapshot shared across all space queries.
Defines DEFAULT_DECISION_SPACES (6 fast spaces) and EXPLAIN_DECISION_SPACES (adds extractions).
Uses a calibrated cross-encoder relevance floor _CTX_FLOOR = -0.13, with dated calibration notes.
Maintains an in-process _THEOREM_CLAIMS_CACHE keyed on (decision_text, vector_version).
Supplies server-side element texts for theorem-fork required elements: ai_uncertainty_caveat and grounding_source.
_provenance_text resolves board:// and code:// addresses, including JSON-path and markdown-section anchors.
explanation_grounding skips the full 7-space recall for theorem-fork or when rerank=False to avoid wasted work.
_rerank_claims calls the local reranker directly with inline claim text rather than addressed corpus hits.
Inline comments are dense, dated, and narrate design history, regressions, and calibration-by-use.
standards: The theorem-fork never-assert law is implemented structurally: a dedicated determine over the theorem asset, per-claim rerank, framework-only block, and explicit AI-projection caveat.
The per-decision bleed fix combines anchoring on the decision's own declared content with a calibrated rerank floor.
The SCANDIR-ONCE + EMBED-ONCE optimization for multi-space recall.
Heavy use of dated lead comments (e.g., 2026-06-21) that record regressions, fixes, and live measurements.
conventions: Follows the reuse-don't-parallel / union law by composing existing corpus stages.
Degrade-clean: down embedders, missing sources, and reranker failures fall back to cosine order or slimmer bundles with honest notes.
Operator-law altitude: surfaces meaning, not machine ids or raw JSON.
Channel-native design: exposes a single shared corpus read for all channels.
Uses underscore-prefixed helpers for internal normalization and rendering.
concerns: Large amounts of historical narrative inline may obscure the executable logic.
_THEOREM_CLAIMS_CACHE is in-process only and is lost on restart.
_CTX_FLOOR is a magic number tied to a specific live reranker scale.
_provenance_text reads files directly from disk rather than through the store abstraction.
Broad exception swallowing in several helpers can hide failure modes.
notes: This is the decision-shaped read of the shared corpus: it retrieves, reranks, resolves meaning, and assembles a grounded explanation bundle, with structural guardrails that keep theorem-fork explanations anchored to the author's own framework.
questions: What is the exact shape of a decision record returned by the decision registry?
How is suite._embed_consult_query implemented, and what happens if it is missing?
How does corpus_rerank.rerank_hits behave when skip_missing=True and some pooled sources lack CAS digests?
What is the relationship between explanation_grounding and territory_prose, and which consumer uses the block string?
How are board:// and code:// addresses normalized before reaching this module?
Under what conditions is prewarm_theorem_explains actually invoked?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the implementation: it is the explain-half memory seam that returns a context bundle from the shared corpus, surfaces legible meaning, and adds theorem-fork grounding guardrails.
apparent_themes: Grounding in the author's own framework
intention_signals: It tries to make decision explanations safe and traceable for a small model by anchoring each explanation in the decision's own authored content, filtering cross-space corpus noise with a calibrated rerank floor, and for theorem decisions forcing a framework-only block extracted from the author's own mathematics with explicit caveats about AI projection.
novelty: The structural never-assert law for theorem-fork decisions, implemented as a dedicated determine+cache+framework-only block plus per-claim reranking, and the per-decision bleed fix that combines self-content anchoring with a calibrated cross-encoder relevance floor.
contribution: Provides a decision-specific memory retrieval and explanation-grounding seam that reuses existing corpus, rerank, neighbour, and determine machinery; attaches human-readable meaning to context items; resolves per-decision provenance from board:// and code:// sources; and adds theorem-fork guardrails so explanations can be traced to the author's own framework.
summary_for_embedding: A runtime module that retrieves and reranks shared-corpus context for a pending decision, resolves legible meaning and provenance, and composes a single explanation-grounding bundle with special framework-only handling for theorem-fork decisions.
intention_for_embedding: To give the RHM a grounded, traceable, decision-shaped memory read that anchors explanations in the author's own content and framework while keeping off-topic corpus bleed out.

symbols.does: Pre-computes theorem-fork explanation groundings at startup to warm the in-process cache.
symbols.description: Iterates the decision registry for subtype='theorem-fork', calls explanation_grounding for each, and populates _THEOREM_CLAIMS_CACHE so repeat opens are fast; best-effort and currently lazy-only.

symbols.does: Retrieves prior decision-shaped corpus records about a topic.
symbols.description: Queries history and common_knowledge spaces with a decision-framed query, optionally reranks the pooled hits, and returns a list of source/space/score items to surface decisions made about this before.

symbols.does: Extracts a human-readable text snippet from a corpus record.
symbols.description: Normalizes various output shapes (string, dict, list) into a short prose digest, skipping empty fields and machine identifiers, so consumers see meaning rather than raw JSON.

symbols.does: Resolves an extraction:// address into a readable summary.
symbols.description: Routes through recall_determine.read_extraction and joins the about, summary, and top claims fields, enabling the extractions grounding space to render meaning instead of a bare address.

symbols.does: Attaches digest text to each context item in-place.
symbols.description: Bulk-fetches the newest corpus records for a list of context sources and calls _clean_meaning (or _extraction_text for extraction:// sources), adding a text key to each item for downstream rendering.

symbols.does: Strips formatting and section markers from a provenance excerpt.
symbols.description: Removes markdown bold/code, heading prefixes, bullets, and leading section-id markers from source text so the operator sees content meaning without document-structure symbols.

symbols.does: Resolves a decision's explanation_source to its origin content.
symbols.description: Handles board:// (title+body) and code:// (whole file, JSON-path anchor, or markdown section anchor) addresses, returning a cleaned, decision-scoped excerpt or empty string if unresolvable.

symbols.does: Builds the cross-space memory context bundle for a decision.
symbols.description: Embeds the decision text once, queries each configured corpus space, optionally reranks the pooled hits, attaches digest text, adds neighbour and prior-decision legs, and returns a degrade-clean bundle with notes.

symbols.does: Reranks raw claim strings by relevance to a query using the jina reranker.
symbols.description: Sends claim text directly to the local reranker service, orders results by cross-encoder score, and degrades to the original order on failure; keyed by index because chunk_id is not unique.

symbols.does: Composes the authoritative explain-turn grounding content for a decision record.
symbols.description: Decides whether to run full corpus recall, tags provenance per context item, runs a theorem-only determine for theorem-fork subtypes with caching and claim reranking, and renders a single operator-legible meaning block combining the decision's own content, provenance, gated corpus context, and prior decisions.
